title: How long does it take to build an Eva
display_title: How long does it take to build an Eva
wikitext:
[]: How long does it take to build an Eva?             Quote Post by LordCaldwell » Mon Feb 22, 2010 6:13 pm In episode 23, one of the Seele counci. Members said "there are now eight Evas that are under construction, and four that are not." 1) if EoE takes place after episode 24, then the nine MP evas must have been completed by then. Since the seele council members statement seems to suggest that the constuction recently began. So, there would have been a matter of weeks for the construction of 9 MP evas to be completed 2) this may just be because of having to make an unplanned movie, but why would seele commision the construction of 12 evas, when they only needed 9? What could the significance of the other four have been? 3) seele: "now that the lance of longinous is lost to us, we must proceed using lilith's only true offspring: Eva unit 01." any speculation on how seele would have used the lol to initiate insturmentality? Perhaps recreate the events of the second impact?

[]: Re: How long does it take to build an Eva?             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Feb 22, 2010 7:56 pm LordCaldwell wrote: why would seele commision the construction of 12 evas, when they only needed 9? What could the significance of the other four have been?

[]: Re: How long does it take to build an Eva?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Feb 22, 2010 8:40 pm LordCaldwell wrote: 2) this may just be because of having to make an unplanned movie, but why would seele commision the construction of 12 evas, when they only needed 9? What could the significance of the other four have been?

[]: Re: How long does it take to build an Eva?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Feb 23, 2010 12:25 pm The Evangelions have probably all been in the initial stages of construction for quite some time. It probably takes years to grow them to full size after all. However, the MP Evangelions have been on hold for three reasons. Firstly, Nerv does not fully understand the technology needed to create them. As witnessed with EVA-00 and EVA-01, there remain significant problems with control, etc. Secondly, Seele is waiting for the S^2 engine technology to become available before completing the MP Evas so that they will not be reliant on umbilical cables. They obtain the first sames of this technology in episode #05, and probably first test it with EVA-04 in episode #17 to disasterous effect. Presumably after this, they manage to debug the system enough that it can be used safely. Thirdly, as evidenced by the Kaworu plugs, the MP Evas are to be controlled by a Dummy Plug system, not by pilots. This was probably always the intention of Seele as allowing Pilots any level of control over Third Impact would probably be—and indeed was—disastrous to Seele's plans. The dummy system only receives its first real test in episode #18, so after this was successful, Seele were finally in a position to complete construction of the Eva Series. Given just how quickly Nerv are able to repair the Evas, and given that the test-type Unit-02 and its machine tools have already been made, I imagine the completion of the MP Evas could have been done quite swiftly. While we only hear about them as late as episode #23, they may have been in production for quite some time. As to the four MP Evas that are not under construction, it must be recalled that parts from some Eva's have been diverted to the repair of the operational units. So perhaps some materials from the presumed EVA-14 were also diverted, while leaving the possibility of its completion by the time of EoE still open. Pertinent Quote  SPOILER: Show

Article: How long does it take to build an Eva
 The Evangelions have probably all been in the initial stages of construction for quite some time. It probably takes years to grow them to full size after all. However, the MP Evangelions have been on hold for three reasons. Firstly, Nerv does not fully understand the technology needed to create them. As witnessed with EVA-00 and EVA-01, there remain significant problems with control, etc. Secondly, Seele is waiting for the S^2 engine technology to become available before completing the MP Evas so that they will not be reliant on umbilical cables. They obtain the first sames of this technology in episode #05, and probably first test it with EVA-04 in episode #17 to disasterous effect. Presumably after this, they manage to debug the system enough that it can be used safely. Thirdly, as evidenced by the Kaworu plugs, the MP Evas are to be controlled by a Dummy Plug system, not by pilots. This was probably always the intention of Seele as allowing Pilots any level of control over Third Impact would probably be—and indeed was—disastrous to Seele's plans. The dummy system only receives its first real test in episode #18, so after this was successful, Seele were finally in a position to complete construction of the Eva Series. Given just how quickly Nerv are able to repair the Evas, and given that the test-type Unit-02 and its machine tools have already been made, I imagine the completion of the MP Evas could have been done quite swiftly. While we only hear about them as late as episode #23, they may have been in production for quite some time. As to the four MP Evas that are not under construction, it must be recalled that parts from some Eva's have been diverted to the repair of the operational units. So perhaps some materials from the presumed EVA-14 were also diverted, while leaving the possibility of its completion by the time of EoE still open. Pertinent Quote  SPOILER: Show
